subject_tmpl: Plow never came down {STREET} — {DATE}
body_tmpl: Hi, I live on {STREET} near {CROSS} and our block has not been plowed at all since the storm on {DATE}. There are at least {NUM} inches of snow still packed down and it is getting dangerous. I have called twice but nobody picks up. Can someone please send a plow out today? My name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I can be reached at {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Mr./Ms. {LAST}, thank you for reaching out. We have added {STREET} between {CROSS} and the next block to today's priority list. A plow should be through by {TIME}. We apologize for the delay — crews are working extended shifts to cover all routes.

subject_tmpl: Icy conditions on {STREET} — someone is going to get hurt
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I am writing because {STREET} near {CROSS} has been a sheet of ice for {DAYS} days now. I watched an elderly man nearly fall this morning trying to get to the bus stop. We pay our taxes and expect safe roads. Please make this a priority before there is a serious accident. — {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Plow took out my mailbox on {DATE}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. Your plow came through early {DATE} and clipped my mailbox clean off the post. The box is lying in the snowbank. I took photos. Who do I contact to file a damage claim? I need this resolved quickly because the postal carrier will not deliver without a mailbox.
reply_tmpl: Mr./Ms. {LAST}, I'm sorry to hear about the damage. Please email photos to Public Works and we will open a claim. Our standard review takes {DAYS} business days. You may also come in to Village Hall at 3200 Washington Blvd to complete a claim form.

subject_tmpl: Windrow blocking my driveway every morning
body_tmpl: I understand plowing has to happen but every single morning the plow dumps a huge windrow right across the end of my driveway at {ADDRESS}. I'm {AGE} years old and I cannot shovel that amount of snow. Is there any way to route the plow so it doesn't pile it at driveways? This is {STREET} between {CROSS} and the alley. Please help.

subject_tmpl: No salt on the hill at {STREET} — Metra commuters at risk
body_tmpl: Mayor, the hill on {STREET} approaching the Metra UP-W station has been unsalted since {DATE}. This morning I saw {NUM} people slip trying to make their train. Many of us walk that hill every weekday. If someone breaks a hip the Village will be liable. Please get a salt truck out there before rush hour tomorrow. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for flagging this safety concern. I've directed Public Works to salt and sand the {STREET} grade near the Metra station this afternoon and again before the morning commute on {DATE}. Appreciate you looking out for your neighbors.

subject_tmpl: Salt request — {STREET} & {CROSS} intersection
body_tmpl: Hi, the intersection of {STREET} and {CROSS} is very slippery this morning. Cars are sliding through the stop sign. Can you please send a salt truck? I drive through here to take my kids to school every morning and it's getting scary. Thank you. {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: We have dispatched a crew to salt {STREET} and {CROSS}. Thank you for the heads-up — please continue to report hazardous spots so we can respond quickly.

subject_tmpl: Plow damage — parkway tree and lawn torn up
body_tmpl: The overnight plow on {DATE} went up onto our parkway at {ADDRESS} and destroyed a section of lawn and damaged the young tree the Village planted last spring. I know conditions are tough but this is real property damage. Please advise me on the damage claim process and whether the Village will replace the tree. Thank you, {FIRST} {LAST}.

subject_tmpl: Thank you — {STREET} was plowed fast after the storm
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I just wanted to say thank you. The crews had {STREET} cleared by {TIME} after last night's storm — that's the fastest I've seen in years. My neighbor is on dialysis and has to leave early for her appointments. Today she made it out without any problem. Please pass along my appreciation to the Public Works team. Sincerely, {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Complaint: plows skipping our block for the third time
body_tmpl: Mayor, our block of {STREET} ({CROSS} to {NUM} {STREET}) has been skipped three storms in a row. I've submitted service requests each time and nothing changes. Meanwhile parallel blocks look perfectly clear. Is there a routing error? Are we on the list? I'm at my wit's end. Please look into this personally. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}
reply_tmpl: Mr./Ms. {LAST}, I've personally pulled your block's plow log and you're right — there was a route mapping error after a driver change last month. It has been corrected. I've asked Frank DiMeo in Public Works to do a follow-up pass today and will call you directly to confirm. I apologize for the frustration.

subject_tmpl: Ice forming from storm drain overflow on {STREET}
body_tmpl: There's a storm drain at {STREET} and {CROSS} that keeps backing up and the water then refreezes across the whole lane. It's been like this since {DATE}. Can public works clear the drain and then salt the ice? I nearly lost control of my car this morning. Address is {ADDRESS} if that helps pinpoint it.

subject_tmpl: Storm response update — {DATE} event
body_tmpl: Karen, here is the post-storm summary for the {DATE} event. We deployed {NUM} plows starting at {TIME}. Primary arterials (St. Charles Rd, Mannheim Rd, Washington Blvd) were cleared by 5 a.m. Residential routes completed by {TIME}. Salt usage: approximately {NUM} tons. Outstanding complaints as of this email: {NUM}. Crew overtime is running about {DAYS} hours total. Let me know if the Mayor needs a briefing.

subject_tmpl: Alley behind {ADDRESS} still completely buried
body_tmpl: Hi, the alley running behind {ADDRESS} on {STREET} has not been touched since the blizzard. Garbage trucks can't get through and residents can't get their cars out. We have at least {NUM} households back there who all need this cleared. Please tell me when the Village plans to do alleys — this is urgent. {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, alley plowing typically begins after all primary and secondary streets are complete. We expect to reach your alley by {DATE}. We appreciate your patience and will add it to the priority list for the next pass.

subject_tmpl: Unsafe snow pile blocking sight lines at {STREET} & {CROSS}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo — a massive pile of snow from repeated plowing has accumulated at the corner of {STREET} and {CROSS}. Drivers pulling out cannot see oncoming traffic at all. I'm worried about a collision. Can the Village arrange to have this pile removed or at least graded down? Thank you, {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Car stuck in unplowed street — need help
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here at {ADDRESS}. My car is completely stuck in the snow in front of my house on {STREET}. I have tried everything. The street is packed with {NUM} inches of unplowed snow. I have a medical appointment at {TIME} today and I desperately need this street cleared or at least a pass in front of my address. Please, {PHONE}.

subject_tmpl: Request for additional salt bin on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Hello, several neighbors on {STREET} near {CROSS} have asked whether the Village would consider placing a community salt bin at our intersection. We have an icy slope here every winter and it would help residents to be able to salt the sidewalk in front of their homes between plow visits. We're willing to help maintain it. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}
reply_tmpl: Thank you for the suggestion, {FIRST}. We'll review the location and our supply situation. If feasible, we can place a bin before the next storm event. I'll follow up within {DAYS} days.

subject_tmpl: Plow driver acting recklessly on {STREET}
body_tmpl: I need to report a plow driver on {STREET} around {TIME} on {DATE} who was going way too fast and fishtailed on the ice, nearly hitting a parked car. The truck number was {NUM}. I understand crews are working hard but this could have been a serious accident. I would like someone to review this incident.
reply_tmpl: I've forwarded your report to Public Works Director DiMeo for immediate review. We take driver safety complaints seriously. If you have any video footage, please share it and reference case {CASE}. Thank you for bringing this to our attention.

subject_tmpl: Snow piled against fire hydrant — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Quick note: the plow has buried the fire hydrant directly in front of {ADDRESS} on {STREET} under a large snowbank. You can't even see the top of it. I figured the fire department might want to know. Just trying to be a good neighbor. {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: When will {STREET} be re-plowed? Repack from overnight
body_tmpl: Hi, {STREET} was plowed yesterday but the snow that blew back overnight and the refreezing has made it almost as bad as before. Particularly the section between {CROSS} and {NUM} Ave. Any chance of a second pass today? I have {NUM} people in my household, two of them elderly. Thanks, {FIRST}

subject_tmpl: Snow emergency parking confusion — got a ticket
body_tmpl: Mayor, I received a parking ticket on {DATE} for a snow emergency violation but I honestly did not know a snow emergency was in effect. I don't see how residents are supposed to find out. I moved my car as soon as I saw the sign. Is there any way to appeal this {AMOUNT} fine? I live at {ADDRESS}. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}
reply_tmpl: Mr./Ms. {LAST}, please contact the Village Clerk's office at Village Hall to request an administrative hearing for ticket {CASE}. You can also sign up for our emergency text alerts at the Village website so you receive future snow emergency notifications automatically.

subject_tmpl: Bus stop on {STREET} completely buried — {DATE}
body_tmpl: The bus stop on {STREET} at {CROSS} is buried under the plow windrow — there's no way to stand there waiting for the bus without being in the travel lane. Several of my neighbors who don't drive depend on that stop. Can someone clear around it? {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, clearing around bus stops is on our list but gets prioritized after lane clearing. We will have a crew address the {STREET} stop at {CROSS} by end of day {DATE}. Thank you.

subject_tmpl: Ice on {STREET} near school crosswalk
body_tmpl: There is a solid sheet of ice at the crosswalk on {STREET} and {CROSS}. Kids from the elementary school cross there every morning. I saw a child fall today. This is a school crossing with a guard — please salt this location first thing tomorrow. I'm very concerned. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Mr./Ms. {LAST}, school crossings are a priority. I've added {STREET} at {CROSS} to tomorrow's pre-dawn salt run. We'll make sure it is treated before students arrive. Thank you for watching out for the children.

subject_tmpl: Pothole emergence on {STREET} — freeze-thaw damage assessment
body_tmpl: Frank, with the current freeze-thaw cycle I've been seeing significant pothole formation on {STREET} between {CROSS} and {NUM} Ave. I'll do a formal damage assessment on {DATE} and submit the list for spring patching. In the meantime a temporary cold-patch crew might be worth dispatching for the worst spots — particularly near the intersection of {STREET} and {CROSS} where the base is compromised. Tom Reyes, P.E.

subject_tmpl: Seniors on {STREET} can't get out — please prioritize
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm writing on behalf of several senior residents on {STREET} between {CROSS} and {NUM} Ave. Many of us are over {AGE} and cannot shovel. The street has not been plowed in {DAYS} days and we are essentially trapped. We have {NUM} residents who need to get to medical appointments this week. Please help us.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for writing. I've asked Public Works to make {STREET} a priority pass today. For seniors who need transportation assistance during a snow emergency, please also contact our Parks and Community Services office — they may be able to coordinate rides for medical appointments.

subject_tmpl: Standing water freezing over on {STREET} — drainage problem
body_tmpl: Hi, on {STREET} near {CROSS} the snowmelt during the day is pooling and then freezing overnight into a massive ice patch that covers the full width of the road. It's a drainage issue that makes the road impassable in the morning. Can you salt and also look at the underlying drainage? This happens every winter. {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Plow blocked end of cul-de-sac on {STREET}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. I live on the cul-de-sac at the end of {STREET}. The plow always dumps everything at the turnaround and then leaves. None of us can get out. Can the driver actually clear the cul-de-sac properly? This is the third storm with the same problem. {PHONE}
reply_tmpl: Mr./Ms. {LAST}, cul-de-sac clearing requires an additional pass with a smaller piece of equipment. We have noted your location and will dispatch the right equipment. You should see the turnaround cleared within {DAYS} hours.

subject_tmpl: Plow noise at 3 AM — is there a noise ordinance?
body_tmpl: Mayor, I understand plows need to run overnight but the truck on {STREET} last night was scraping the pavement with what sounded like a steel blade at full force from {TIME} until almost {TIME}. My whole family woke up including a newborn baby. Is there a quieter approach or any consideration given to residential areas at that hour? {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Icy sidewalk near Metra station — {DATE} morning
body_tmpl: I commute every morning from Bellwood's Metra UP-W station. The sidewalk on {STREET} between {CROSS} and the station entrance is a solid sheet of ice this morning ({DATE}). I watched {NUM} people nearly fall. Who is responsible for salting that stretch? The Village or Metra? Either way someone needs to act today. {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Snow plow cracked my windshield
body_tmpl: Good morning, I was driving on {STREET} behind a Village plow on {DATE} around {TIME} when the truck threw a large chunk of ice that cracked my windshield. I have the damage documented and the repair estimate is {AMOUNT}. I would like to file a claim against the Village. Please tell me the process. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}
reply_tmpl: Mr./Ms. {LAST}, please submit your claim to the Village Manager's office at Village Hall, 3200 Washington Blvd, along with your estimate and photos. You'll receive a claim number within {DAYS} business days. Liability determinations are handled case by case.

subject_tmpl: Why is Mannheim Rd clear but my street is not?
body_tmpl: Mayor, I see that Mannheim Road is perfectly clear and salted but {STREET} right behind it hasn't seen a plow in two days. I get that main roads come first but {DAYS} days for a residential street seems excessive. I have {NUM} children I'm trying to get to school. Can you explain the priority system? {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, Mannheim Road is a state route maintained by IDOT, not the Village. Our own crews prioritize Village arterials like St. Charles Rd and Washington Blvd first, then residential streets. {STREET} should be reached within the next {DAYS} hours. I understand the frustration and I'm monitoring crew progress.

subject_tmpl: Contractor pile blocking road on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Hi, a contractor appears to have piled snow from a commercial lot onto {STREET} near {CROSS} and now it's narrowing the lane to one car width. I don't know who the contractor is. Can the Village inspect and require them to remove it? It happened sometime the night of {DATE}. My address is {ADDRESS}. {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Great job by the crew this morning — wish I'd gotten his name
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, quick appreciation note. A plow driver on {STREET} this morning saw that I was stuck in the snow and actually stopped and helped me figure out how to maneuver out. He didn't have to do that. He had the truck number {NUM}. He was professional and genuinely kind. Please pass this along. These folks work so hard. {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: What a wonderful message to receive — thank you, {FIRST}. I know exactly who you're describing and I will personally pass your thanks along. Our Public Works crew shows up in the hardest conditions and a note like this means the world to them.

subject_tmpl: Black ice on {STREET} — no salt visible anywhere
body_tmpl: There is black ice on {STREET} from {CROSS} to {NUM} Ave as of {TIME} this morning. You cannot see it and cars are sliding. I counted {NUM} near-misses in ten minutes. There is zero salt anywhere on this stretch. Please dispatch immediately — this is extremely dangerous. {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: We're sending a salt truck to {STREET} right now. Black ice reports go to the top of the queue. Please stay safe and thank you for calling it in. We'll make an additional check at {TIME}.

subject_tmpl: PD request — salt {STREET} ASAP — accident scene cleared
body_tmpl: Frank, we just cleared a two-car fender-bender at {STREET} and {CROSS} — black ice was the cause. PD is requesting immediate salt and possibly a sand spread on that section. Scene is clear and the road is open. Time is {TIME}. Thanks — Ofc. Banks

subject_tmpl: How do I report an unsalted street?
body_tmpl: Hi, I've been searching the Village website but can't find a clear way to report icy or unsalted roads. Can you give me the right phone number, email, or online form? The street I need to report is {STREET} near {CROSS}. It's been slippery since {DATE}. I just want to make sure I'm reaching the right department. {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, you can reach Public Works directly at the number on our website or email pw@bellwoodil.gov. You can also call Village Hall at 3200 Washington Blvd. I'm logging your report for {STREET} near {CROSS} right now and a crew will check it today.

subject_tmpl: Road condition on {STREET} worse than last winter
body_tmpl: Mayor, I've lived on {STREET} for {DAYS} years and I can say with confidence that snow removal this winter is worse than last year. It seems like there are fewer plows out, or the routes are longer. Is there a budget issue? Have we lost equipment or staff? I'd appreciate a straightforward answer about what is happening. {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Salt brine wash rusting my car — concern
body_tmpl: Hi, I realize salt keeps roads safe but the amount of brine being applied on {STREET} is leaving a heavy residue on every car in the neighborhood. My {AGE}-year-old vehicle is already showing rust and I'm worried about long-term damage. Is there an alternative de-icing product the Village has considered? Just asking. {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Disabled resident needs priority plow pass — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hello, I am submitting this request on behalf of my neighbor at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. She is wheelchair-dependent and the snow and ice in front of her home has trapped her inside for {DAYS} days. Her caregiver cannot get in either. Is there any priority service available for residents with disabilities? {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}
reply_tmpl: Thank you for advocating for your neighbor. We do have a vulnerability protocol — please call us directly at {PHONE} and we will schedule a priority pass and sidewalk clearing at {ADDRESS}. We appreciate you reaching out.

subject_tmpl: Pre-treatment request before next storm — {DATE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, the forecast is calling for freezing rain on {DATE}. Last time we got freezing rain the Village didn't pre-treat and the roads were terrible for days. Is there a plan to apply brine before the storm hits? Especially on {STREET} which is a slope and always ices up first. Just hoping to avoid a repeat. {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Good thinking. I've confirmed with Public Works that pre-treatment runs are scheduled for the evening of {DATE} on primary routes, including {STREET}. We learned from last time. Stay safe and thank you for staying engaged.

subject_tmpl: Street light out on icy stretch of {STREET}
body_tmpl: There's a street light out on {STREET} at {CROSS} and with the ice and snow the dark section is really hazardous — you can't see the road conditions until you're on them. Can you report this to whoever handles street lights and also maybe get some salt on that stretch? {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}

subject_tmpl: Plow blocking {BIZ} customer access — {DATE}
body_tmpl: This is {NAME} at {BIZ}, {ADDRESS} on St. Charles Rd. The overnight plow has left a large berm of snow directly in front of our entrance and customers cannot safely walk from the street to our door. We have lost business two mornings in a row. Can a crew come clear the access? We have no equipment to move this amount of snow ourselves. {PHONE}
reply_tmpl: Hello, we will dispatch a crew to clear the access at {ADDRESS} today. Commercial frontage on St. Charles Rd is on our secondary pass schedule. Going forward you may also contact us by {TIME} and we can try to accommodate same-day clearing requests.

subject_tmpl: Snow removal schedule — where can I find it?
body_tmpl: Hi, can you point me to where I can find the Village's snow removal route schedule? I want to understand what priority tier {STREET} is in so I know roughly when to expect the plow. I'm also curious whether there's a tracker or at least an estimated time published somewhere. {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, our snow removal priority map is posted on the Village website under Public Works. {STREET} is a Tier {NUM} residential route, which means it gets plowed after arterials are complete — typically within {DAYS} hours of storm end. We don't have a real-time tracker yet but we post updates on social media.

subject_tmpl: Neighbor pushing snow into the street — {STREET}
body_tmpl: Mayor, my neighbor at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} keeps using a snowblower to push all their snow into the middle of the street, which is illegal I believe. After the plow comes through it leaves a huge mound that makes the lane too narrow. I've asked them to stop and they ignored me. Can code enforcement address this? {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Urgent: Metra station hill not salted — {DATE} a.m.
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here. The grade on {STREET} going up to the Metra UP-W station has not been salted this morning and it is {DATE}. The {TIME} train is in {NUM} minutes and commuters are already sliding on the hill trying to get there. Someone needs to get out there right now. My cell is {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: We are dispatching a salt truck to {STREET} at the Metra grade immediately. Estimated arrival {NUM} minutes. Thank you for the urgent alert — we'll add this slope to the pre-dawn salt checklist.

subject_tmpl: Plow missed {STREET} entirely on {DATE}
body_tmpl: Hi there, I want to verify that {STREET} between {CROSS} and {NUM} Ave was actually on the plow route on {DATE}. The street looked completely untouched all day. I drove the whole block and there's no evidence of a plow pass — the snow is still in its original storm pattern. Is there a log I can check? {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Appreciation — fastest plow response I've seen in Bellwood
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I just have to say — after the {DATE} storm, {STREET} was cleared by {TIME} in the morning. I've been a resident for {DAYS} years and I don't think I've ever seen that kind of response time. Whatever you and the Public Works team did this winter, please keep it up. This village is improving and I notice. Thank you. {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Thank you so much, {FIRST}. Messages like yours make a real difference to our crew members who work through the night in difficult conditions. I'll share your kind words with Director DiMeo and the whole team.
